title: Migrate smart-router to async-iterator decision model and upgrade protobufs
preview: Background: The current smart-router evaluates policy decisions in a synchronous, lock-heavy control path that exacerbates tail latency under bursty traffic and makes incremental changes (new fallback adapters and regional policy rules) risky. The protobuf contract for decision blobs is also split across two formats...
text:
Background: The current smart-router evaluates policy decisions in a synchronous, lock-heavy control path that exacerbates tail latency under bursty traffic and makes incremental changes (new fallback adapters and regional policy rules) risky. The protobuf contract for decision blobs is also split across two formats which complicates caching and cross-region fallback compatibility.

Goal: Replace the sync decision pipeline with an async-iterator based evaluation model that allows streaming policy decisions and progressive fallbacks (first-fast, then-complete), and standardize the decision protobuf to a single backward-compatible v5 schema. This reduces lock contention, short-circuits cold-paths, and simplifies retry/fallback logic.

Scope:
- Introduce AsyncDecisionIterator interface and migrate hotpath policy evaluators (affinity, capacity, SLA checks) to yield partial results.
- Impl...

title: Stride-aware watermark coalescer for mixed-length prompt/response streams
preview: Background: Our continuous batching runtime currently struggles with mixed prompt/response workloads where sequences interleave short system prompts and long user responses. We observe two recurring issues: (1) KV cache residency spikes and fragmentation that cause out-of-memory (OOM) tail cases on smaller GPUs duri...
text:
Background: Our continuous batching runtime currently struggles with mixed prompt/response workloads where sequences interleave short system prompts and long user responses. We observe two recurring issues: (1) KV cache residency spikes and fragmentation that cause out-of-memory (OOM) tail cases on smaller GPUs during spikes, and (2) suboptimal slot utilization because the scheduler either waits too long for long-turn arrivals or coalesces too aggressively, increasing latency for short-turn requests.

Goal: Design and ship a scheduler tuning that adaptively coalesces requests using a stride-aware watermarking strategy and coordinated KV stripe compaction to improve GPU memory utilization and reduce p95/p99 latency for tail-heavy mixed-length traffic while keeping median latency neutral or improved.

Key ideas: (1) Stride-aware watermarks: replace the single static windowing threshold ...

title: Stochastic ensemble calibration and post-quantization bias correction for E4M3/E5M2 on Hopper
preview: Enable a robust FP8 activation selection pipeline for Hopper (E4M3/E5M2) by combining multiple stochastic calibration seeds, an ensemble scoring layer, and a lightweight post-quantization bias-correction pass. This ticket addresses edge-case regressions observed in long-tail prompt families and conversational contex...
text: Enable a robust FP8 activation selection pipeline for Hopper (E4M3/E5M2) by combining multiple stochastic calibration seeds, an ensemble scoring layer, and a lightweight post-quantization bias-correction pass. This ticket addresses edge-case regressions observed in long-tail prompt families and conversational context drift when using single-seed calibration. The aim is to reduce worst-case accuracy drops while keeping latency impact within SLOs.
links: GitHub PR: redwood/runtime#9876 (ensemble scorer + shim kernels)
Dashboard: Console > Models > hopper-e4m3-e5m2 > canary (telemetry view)

title: Customer-fidelity load generator for Private/VPC reproductions
preview: Build a reproducible, configurable load generator that can emulate customer traffic and environment characteristics for Private/VPC/on-prem Redwood deployments. The harness must produce stable, repeatable tokens/sec profiles and p95 latency distributions so we can attribute regressions to runtime/kernel/scheduling c...
text: Build a reproducible, configurable load generator that can emulate customer traffic and environment characteristics for Private/VPC/on-prem Redwood deployments. The harness must produce stable, repeatable tokens/sec profiles and p95 latency distributions so we can attribute regressions to runtime/kernel/scheduling changes rather than test noise. Key features: replay of snapped request traces, synthetic traffic distributions (session lengths, user think-time, prompt prefixes), KV-cache warmup profiles, N-to-1 routing for multi-tenant simulations, network condition emulation (bandwidth, latency, jitter), and an automated regression detector that compares current runs to baseline runs and opens a ticket when drift exceeds thresholds. Outputs must integrate with Prometheus/Grafana and Redwood Console dashboards (token/sec, p95, p99, error-rate, cost-per-token).

title: Quantization matrix intake and SLA escrow for perf regression tracking
preview: Create a structured intake and validation flow for quantization profiles (INT8/FP8/GPTQ/AWQ) that ties each profile to a pinable model-version, publishes a standardized performance profile, and enrolls the artifact in an SLA-backed regression escrow. This ticket focuses on the intake contract, automated smoke checks...
text: Create a structured intake and validation flow for quantization profiles (INT8/FP8/GPTQ/AWQ) that ties each profile to a pinable model-version, publishes a standardized performance profile, and enrolls the artifact in an SLA-backed regression escrow. This ticket focuses on the intake contract, automated smoke checks, and the escrow mechanics that prevent accidental promotion of regressing artifacts.

title: Strata Shadow Allocator and Idle Migration playbook for KV fragmentation reduction
preview: Problem: Continuous batching workloads create heterogeneous KV entry sizes and lifetimes. Over time this leads to interleaved small and large KV regions, internal fragmentation, and wasted GPU memory that reduces effective model capacity and forces more frequent costly fallbacks. Existing compaction/defrag approache...
text:
Problem: Continuous batching workloads create heterogeneous KV entry sizes and lifetimes. Over time this leads to interleaved small and large KV regions, internal fragmentation, and wasted GPU memory that reduces effective model capacity and forces more frequent costly fallbacks. Existing compaction/defrag approaches (epochal rewrites, eager slab coalescing) either cause high tail latency when triggered or require large pauses.

Proposal: Implement a Strata Shadow Allocator (SSA) plus Idle Migration playbook. SSA organizes the KV arena into lightweight strata driven by per-worker sequence-length histograms and recent access signatures. Instead of moving live active entries synchronously, we maintain a shadow indirection map that lets the allocator point hot lookups to a compacted target region while the real relocation happens opportunistically during idle GPU cycles. Parallel compone...

title: Route consistency window during peering promote causes transient resolver-binding drift
preview: Summary: During a peering promote (when a secondary peering session is promoted to primary to balance load or recover capacity), some Dedicated tenant routes can appear to be consistent at the control plane level while dataplane bindings (egress resolver affinity and next-hop for SNAT) lag by a small window (1-30s)....
text: Summary: During a peering promote (when a secondary peering session is promoted to primary to balance load or recover capacity), some Dedicated tenant routes can appear to be consistent at the control plane level while dataplane bindings (egress resolver affinity and next-hop for SNAT) lag by a small window (1-30s). The lag can cause tenant traffic to be forwarded to an egress path that no longer has the correct resolver mapping, producing transient DNS misresolution and connection resets for long-lived client sessions. Several customer escalations reported 5-30s spikes of 500-level proxy errors and DNS NXDOMAINs coincident with peering promotions during scheduled maintenance or autoscaler-driven reparent events.

title: Terraform KMS/HSM lifecycle operator for private deployments
preview: Build a Terraform-based lifecycle operator (module + orchestration patterns) for integrating KMS and HSM backends into Redwood Private deployments. The operator will provide idempotent provisioning, zero-downtime key rotation orchestration, IAM binding bootstrapping, and a safe path for migrating secrets between Sec...
text: Build a Terraform-based lifecycle operator (module + orchestration patterns) for integrating KMS and HSM backends into Redwood Private deployments. The operator will provide idempotent provisioning, zero-downtime key rotation orchestration, IAM binding bootstrapping, and a safe path for migrating secrets between Secret Manager and Vault in air-gapped and VPC environments. This ticket covers the Terraform module design, integration tests, CI gates, and an upgrade strategy for existing on-prem clusters.

title: Regional retention routing matrix and customer blocklist
preview: Goal: Define and implement a canonical routing matrix that enforces EU data residency and retention constraints while supporting customer-scoped blocklists (blocklist = explicit customer/tenant prohibition for specific regions or tiers). This ticket covers schema, runtime enforcement points (API gateway, routing lay...
text: Goal: Define and implement a canonical routing matrix that enforces EU data residency and retention constraints while supporting customer-scoped blocklists (blocklist = explicit customer/tenant prohibition for specific regions or tiers). This ticket covers schema, runtime enforcement points (API gateway, routing layer, and model-serving adapters), operator controls, and migration of existing customer mappings. We must support: 1) 'EU-only' customers (requests must stay inside EU regions), 2) residency-preferred customers (prefer EU but can failover under operator-approved conditions), 3) blocklisted customers that cannot be routed to specific tiers or regions, and 4) mapping to model variants for fallback (e.g., quantized local variant vs hosted large variant). This spec must be implementable in hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments.

title: Modernize CI build system: small base images, build cache, and toolchain bumps
preview:
Summary: Reduce CI build times, lower container image size/CVEs, and improve developer iteration speed by modernizing Docker base images, enabling persistent build caching, and upgrading linters/toolchains across service repos.

Background: Over the last 18 months our monorepo and multiple service repos have accumul...
text:
Summary: Reduce CI build times, lower container image size/CVEs, and improve developer iteration speed by modernizing Docker base images, enabling persistent build caching, and upgrading linters/toolchains across service repos.

Background: Over the last 18 months our monorepo and multiple service repos have accumulated slow CI jobs, large Docker images (some >1.2GB), and stale toolchains (old Node, Go, Python images and linter versions). This increases developer cycle time, costs in GitHub Actions minutes and container storage, and surface area for CVEs.

Scope:
- Replace legacy base images (debian:buster, node:14-slim) with slim/pinned or distroless images where possible and publish SHA-digested images to GHCR.
- Standardize multistage Dockerfiles with BuildKit best-practices across services (cache mounts, stage ordering, node_modules caching).
- Enable remote build cache for GitHub...

title: Passive-region warmup validation and circuit-breaker probing
preview: Objective: Create and validate a deterministic warmup and probe regimen for passive regions to ensure fast activation with bounded risk. We will automate traffic-probing and circuit-breaker exercise sequences, verify backup/restore integrity, and exercise load-shedding rules under controlled conditions. Focus is on ...
text: Objective: Create and validate a deterministic warmup and probe regimen for passive regions to ensure fast activation with bounded risk. We will automate traffic-probing and circuit-breaker exercise sequences, verify backup/restore integrity, and exercise load-shedding rules under controlled conditions. Focus is on measurable SLO impact, observable rollback criteria, and a reproducible runbook.

title: runtime-configurator-for-policy-based-retention-and-evidence-streaming
preview: Implement a runtime-configurable TTL allocator and evidence-streaming pipeline that derives retention for prompts, responses, request logs, and metrics from an authoritative policy set. The feature should support per-tenant, per-route, and per-region derivation, allow deterministic purge orchestration, and emit cont...
text: Implement a runtime-configurable TTL allocator and evidence-streaming pipeline that derives retention for prompts, responses, request logs, and metrics from an authoritative policy set. The feature should support per-tenant, per-route, and per-region derivation, allow deterministic purge orchestration, and emit continuous evidence manifests to a region-aware archive for audits. This ticket covers the runtime components, API surface for policy overrides, streaming hooks for live evidence delivery, and operational rollback controls.

title: Redesign paged ranking envelopes and batchstream semantics for rerank API
preview: Problem: The current reranking endpoint mixes streaming, page cursors, and top_k semantics in ways that make client implementation error-prone for large result sets and causes inconsistent latencies for batched rerank workloads. This ticket redesigns the request/response envelope ("paged ranking envelopes") and intr...
text: Problem: The current reranking endpoint mixes streaming, page cursors, and top_k semantics in ways that make client implementation error-prone for large result sets and causes inconsistent latencies for batched rerank workloads. This ticket redesigns the request/response envelope ("paged ranking envelopes") and introduces an explicit batchstream mode to decouple per-request latency from large-K retrievals. Goals: improve ergonomics for clients, make pagination and stable continuation tokens deterministic across reranker variants, and reduce tail latency for high-concurrency embedding+rerank pipelines.

title: Elastic priority tiers and cross-region spillover admission controller
preview: Design and implement an admission controller that combines elastic priority tiers with cross-region spillover to enforce SLOs under extreme load. Must support per-tenant priorities, dynamic demotion/promotion based on recent SLO burn, and graceful degradation modes (cache-first, context trimming, quantized-model fal...
text: Design and implement an admission controller that combines elastic priority tiers with cross-region spillover to enforce SLOs under extreme load. Must support per-tenant priorities, dynamic demotion/promotion based on recent SLO burn, and graceful degradation modes (cache-first, context trimming, quantized-model fallback, canned stubs).

title: OpenAI Chat Completions compatibility orchestrator: replayable snapshot + request/response shape translator
preview: Problem: Customers calling Redwood with clients written for the OpenAI Chat Completions (CC) API expect identical request/response shapes, streaming chunk semantics, and deterministic function/tool calling behavior. We observe differences in: role/name field contractions, partial stream chunk boundaries for function...
text: Problem: Customers calling Redwood with clients written for the OpenAI Chat Completions (CC) API expect identical request/response shapes, streaming chunk semantics, and deterministic function/tool calling behavior. We observe differences in: role/name field contractions, partial stream chunk boundaries for function calls, and non-deterministic toolcall attribution when we fall back to truncated model variants. This ticket implements an orchestrator that translates inbound OpenAI-CC requests into canonical Redwood runtime shapes, captures a replayable snapshot of request+prefix-state for deterministic replay (debugging/QA), and reconstitutes CC-compatible streaming and fn_call frames on the response path.

title: On-Call Baton Map & Contextual Responder Hub
preview: Design a lightweight, persistent "baton" visualization and accompanying "Responder Hub" surface that reduces context-switching during shift handoffs and incident escalation. The baton map should present a timezone-aware summary of current owners, last actions, and immediate next steps; the Responder Hub is a compact...
text: Design a lightweight, persistent "baton" visualization and accompanying "Responder Hub" surface that reduces context-switching during shift handoffs and incident escalation. The baton map should present a timezone-aware summary of current owners, last actions, and immediate next steps; the Responder Hub is a compact panel surfaced on alerts, timeline items, and the incident drawer that wires to postmortems, runbook snippets, and relevant SLO/cost deltas. Goals: speed up handoffs, reduce duplicate investigation, and make rollouts/alerts continuity explicit across shifts.

title: Cross-plane rapid regression failover and customer triage
preview: Summary: During a staged rollout of runtime-1.19 model variant v2 we observed a correlated spike in tail latency and QA-quality regressions (hallucination score delta ~+12%) on a small subset of tenants across Hosted and Dedicated. This ticket coordinates a rapid cross-plane mitigation: immediate customer triage, ca...
text:
Summary: During a staged rollout of runtime-1.19 model variant v2 we observed a correlated spike in tail latency and QA-quality regressions (hallucination score delta ~+12%) on a small subset of tenants across Hosted and Dedicated. This ticket coordinates a rapid cross-plane mitigation: immediate customer triage, canary quarantine, KV-cache reconciliation, and a staged hotpatch path that preserves tenant isolation for Private deployments.

Goals:
- Contain user-visible regressions within affected tenant slices without a broad rollback.
- Restore baseline quality & latency for high-visibility customers within a 4-hour window where possible.
- Produce an auditable rollback/hotpatch sequence and customer communication for post-incident RCA.

Scope: Hosted API, Redwood Dedicated pools, and Redwood Private (VPC) customers. Includes playbook steps for automated routing rules, NGINX/TLS fron...

title: Rationalize Terraform layering and accelerate plan execution
preview: Background: Over the last 18 months we've accumulated several ad-hoc Terraform overlay patterns and tightly coupled provider initializations that slow down plan/apply runs and create frequent merge conflicts. This ticket scopes a focused refactor to rationalize the module layering, introduce a small compatibility sh...
text:
Background: Over the last 18 months we've accumulated several ad-hoc Terraform overlay patterns and tightly coupled provider initializations that slow down plan/apply runs and create frequent merge conflicts. This ticket scopes a focused refactor to rationalize the module layering, introduce a small compatibility shim for legacy overlays, and add parallelized plan execution and provider init improvements to reduce CI runtime and developer iteration time.

Goals:
- Split monolithic environment modules into clear logical layers (networking -> platform -> services) with well-defined inputs/outputs.
- Introduce a lightweight compatibility shim module to surface old overlay inputs while migrating consumers incrementally.
- Reduce terraform plan latency in CI by enabling targeted parallelization for independent workspaces and optimizing provider init (provider version pinning, partial init ...

title: Multi-tenant burst allocator with soft eviction playbook
preview: Problem: High-frequency burst traffic from a small set of tenants occasionally exhausts local quota tokens and triggers hard rejects or cross-region failovers. When the central limiter or its lease-store experiences transient outages, we currently fall back to a conservative deny-all behavior that leads to large SLO...
text: Problem: High-frequency burst traffic from a small set of tenants occasionally exhausts local quota tokens and triggers hard rejects or cross-region failovers. When the central limiter or its lease-store experiences transient outages, we currently fall back to a conservative deny-all behavior that leads to large SLO violations for affected customers. Goal: implement a resilient multi-tenant burst allocator that gracefully degrades under limiter outages and sustained overloads: - allow short, proportionally-weighted burst absorption using a token escrow backed by per-tenant standing reservations - introduce a soft-eviction policy to preempt low-priority work rather than hard reject high-priority requests - integrate with circuit-breaker signals so allocator behavior automatically widens or tightens depending on downstream health - provide deterministic admission behavior during global ...

title: Q1 2026 perf baseline: kickoff, scope, and execution umbrella
preview:
## Why we’re doing this
We’re entering Q1 2026 with increasing variance risk: more regions, more GPU SKUs, and more customer traffic shapes (streaming + long-context + tool calling + embeddings). This umbrella tracks the end-to-end work to establish:

1) A repeatable benchmarking program (CI-backed) that is reproduc...
text:
## Why we’re doing this
We’re entering Q1 2026 with increasing variance risk: more regions, more GPU SKUs, and more customer traffic shapes (streaming + long-context + tool calling + embeddings). This umbrella tracks the end-to-end work to establish:

1) A repeatable benchmarking program (CI-backed) that is reproducible, attributable, and production-aligned.
2) A Q1 2026 “golden” baseline for top deployed models on key hardware/regions.
3) A representative workload suite v1 we can run in perf-canary + release gating.

Non-goal for this phase: external marketing benchmarks. This is internal baseline + release signoff tooling first.

## Deliverables (end of Q1)
- Baseline report (internal): model x region x GPU matrix + key takeaways + known cliffs + methodology
- Representative workload suite v1:
 - Chat streaming
 - Long-context prefill stress (summarization-like)
 - Tool/function cal...

title: Quantization hysteresis and batch interplay: long-warmup FP8/INT8 throughput & tail-latency study
preview: Background: Recent calibration runs for FP8/INT8 variants show non-monotonic throughput and tail-latency when we vary batch size and long-context windows. We suspect hysteresis in kernel selection + KV-cache warmup creates transient regions where inferred latency spikes and throughput reduces for a sustained period ...
text: Background: Recent calibration runs for FP8/INT8 variants show non-monotonic throughput and tail-latency when we vary batch size and long-context windows. We suspect hysteresis in kernel selection + KV-cache warmup creates transient regions where inferred latency spikes and throughput reduces for a sustained period (tens of seconds) after a burst. Goal: produce reproducible microbenchmarks and end-to-end harness results that characterize (1) steady-state throughput across batch sizes and sequence lengths, (2) transient tail-latency after microbursts (warmup), and (3) points where kernel fallback triggers and causes accuracy/perf trade-offs. The results will feed a mitigation plan (runtime heuristics, calibration policy changes, and an autoscaler tuning doc).

title: SDK v1→v2 Migration: Streaming ergonomics, retry semantics, and changelog automation checklist
preview:
Summary:
This ticket captures a cross-language migration playbook for SDK v1 -> v2 focusing on: (1) streaming ergonomics changes (pull vs push, chunk framing, cancellation), (2) retry and idempotency semantics (header conventions, transient vs terminal errors, backoff policy), and (3) automated changelog/deprecation...
text:
Summary:
This ticket captures a cross-language migration playbook for SDK v1 -> v2 focusing on: (1) streaming ergonomics changes (pull vs push, chunk framing, cancellation), (2) retry and idempotency semantics (header conventions, transient vs terminal errors, backoff policy), and (3) automated changelog/deprecation extraction to keep docs and migration guides in sync. The goal is a developer-friendly migration path that minimizes client breakage while enabling the cleaner v2 interfaces we shipped in the runtime.

Why this matters:
- Many customers embed streaming in UI flows; breaking ergonomics lead to hard-to-debug UI bugs.
- Retry semantics changed between lib versions: previously retries were best-effort on some codepaths; v2 centralizes retry policy in the transport layer which changes error surface and event ordering.
- We must ensure changelogs and deprecation notices are auto...

title: Burst capacity synthesizer for interactive spikes — proportional GPU headroom across weights/KV/activations
preview:
Problem: Interactive tails (short, high-QPS bursts) cause sudden KV and activation pressure that leads to tail latency spikes or evictions of hot weights on multi-tenant GPUs. Our current static headroom buckets are either too conservative (wasted capacity) or too aggressive (evictions under microbursts).

Goal: Des...
text:
Problem: Interactive tails (short, high-QPS bursts) cause sudden KV and activation pressure that leads to tail latency spikes or evictions of hot weights on multi-tenant GPUs. Our current static headroom buckets are either too conservative (wasted capacity) or too aggressive (evictions under microbursts).

Goal: Design and implement a Burst Capacity Synthesizer (BCS) that creates a proportional ephemeral headroom fabric across weights, KV cache, and activations to safely absorb interactive spikes while minimizing steady-state waste. The synthesizer should integrate with continuous batching and the memory ledger so decisions are reversible and observable.

Approach summary:
- Build an Ephemeral Reserve Ledger (ERL) that tracks transient "burst tokens" per tenant at 1s resolution. Tokens are funded by forecasted slack and reclaimed automatically.
- Compute proportional reserves across t...

title: Chronicle Atlas: Micro-journey Mapping & Cost Lensing Prototype
preview: Goal: Prototype a compact, context-aware navigation overlay (Chronicle Atlas) that surfaces micro-journeys across Console surfaces (Dashboards, Rollouts, Cost Views) and folds in a lightweight cost-lensing control. The Atlas is intended to reduce cognitive load when switching contexts, surface relevant rollouts and ...
text: Goal: Prototype a compact, context-aware navigation overlay (Chronicle Atlas) that surfaces micro-journeys across Console surfaces (Dashboards, Rollouts, Cost Views) and folds in a lightweight cost-lensing control. The Atlas is intended to reduce cognitive load when switching contexts, surface relevant rollouts and cost metrics inline, and provide predictable routing behavior for multi-workspace customers. Scope: IA sketch -> mid-fidelity prototype -> internal usability session -> iterate on placement/interaction. Out of scope for this ticket: backend telemetry plumbing, RBAC enforcement, and Dedicated deployment config view changes (these are follow-ups tracked under ENG tickets).

title: Multiprotocol internal ingress terraform module: TLS passthrough + termination, cert fabric, health orchestration
preview: Create a single Terraform module that provides a multiprotocol internal ingress for Private deployments. The module must support: (1) NLB listeners for TLS passthrough to customer backends, (2) ALB for HTTP(s) termination where we need path/host routing and WAF integration, (3) dynamic certificate selection via ACM ...
text: Create a single Terraform module that provides a multiprotocol internal ingress for Private deployments. The module must support: (1) NLB listeners for TLS passthrough to customer backends, (2) ALB for HTTP(s) termination where we need path/host routing and WAF integration, (3) dynamic certificate selection via ACM and Vault-stored certs (cert fabric), (4) configurable session affinity (cookie + source IP fallbacks), and (5) active health orchestration to minimize cross-AZ failover blast radius during upgrades. This ticket owns the module design, provider-agnostic Terraform code (AWS-first), integration tests, upgrade/rollout strategy, and automated healthcheck tooling hooks for Redwood Private packages.

title: Hotpatch and follow-ups for allocator fragmentation and session-cache corruption causing cross-environment GPU OOMs
preview: Summary: Multiple customers across Hosted, Dedicated and Private reported intermittent GPU OOMs over the last three weeks. Investigation shows two correlated runtime problems: (1) allocator fragmentation and small-allocation hoarding under long-lived model sessions, and (2) session-level in-memory cache corruption t...
text: Summary: Multiple customers across Hosted, Dedicated and Private reported intermittent GPU OOMs over the last three weeks. Investigation shows two correlated runtime problems: (1) allocator fragmentation and small-allocation hoarding under long-lived model sessions, and (2) session-level in-memory cache corruption that prevents proper eviction and compaction, allowing KV state to grow without bound. Together these trigger OOMs on high-utilization nodes.

title: Compact clustered view and gesture-friendly column rails for large virtual tables
preview: Summary: Introduce a compact 'clustered' table density mode and a new gesture-friendly column-rail control to improve usability for very high-row-count views on touch and pointer devices. The clustered mode visually groups adjacent rows with small separators and semantic micro-headers to reduce perceived height whil...
text: Summary: Introduce a compact 'clustered' table density mode and a new gesture-friendly column-rail control to improve usability for very high-row-count views on touch and pointer devices. The clustered mode visually groups adjacent rows with small separators and semantic micro-headers to reduce perceived height while preserving scanability. The column rail is a narrow draggable affordance optimized for touch targets and momentum-aware resizing to avoid accidental full-width snaps. This ticket covers design tokens, component API surface, accessibility behavior, and handoff artifacts for engineering and PM.

title: Quant exception litmus run for FinSolve low-latency trading model
preview: Support ticket from customer FinSolve requesting an exception to allow FP8 quantization for their low-latency trading ranking model (llama-2-13b-custom) despite our default policy that prefers INT8 for this model family. Customer reports cost and latency constraints and requests a bespoke calibration run with their ...
text: Support ticket from customer FinSolve requesting an exception to allow FP8 quantization for their low-latency trading ranking model (llama-2-13b-custom) despite our default policy that prefers INT8 for this model family. Customer reports cost and latency constraints and requests a bespoke calibration run with their anonymized seed set and a short litmus evaluation to validate that accuracy stays within a 1.5% regression window on risk-sensitive signals. This ticket tracks engineering work to run the calibration, evaluate accuracy regressions on customer-provided and internal holdout sets, and produce a recommended quant-profile or a safe rollback policy. Deliverables: calibration artifacts, accuracy report, suggested quant-profile override YAML, support playbook for approval and follow-up.

title: Introduce export lineage tokens and TTL-based dedupe to eliminate transient duplicate invoice lines
preview: Problem: Intermittent retry storms and race windows in the hourly export pipeline occasionally create pseudo-duplicate invoice lines in downstream Stripe/NetSuite exports. These duplicates are typically transient (same semantic usage but different export IDs or timestamps) and cause manual reconciliation work and cu...
text:
Problem: Intermittent retry storms and race windows in the hourly export pipeline occasionally create pseudo-duplicate invoice lines in downstream Stripe/NetSuite exports. These duplicates are typically transient (same semantic usage but different export IDs or timestamps) and cause manual reconciliation work and customer confusion. Existing mitigations (watermarking and deterministic merge) have reduced frequency but do not prevent duplicates that emerge from out-of-order leader restarts, partial backfills, or multi-worker write races.

Goal: Add end-to-end lineage tokens to usage records produced by the ingestion tier and carry that token through aggregation and export. Use a TTL-based dedupe table in the export reconciler to detect semantically-equivalent lines within a configurable time window (default 48 hours). Ensure dedupe checks are idempotent and crash-recovery safe. Provide...

title: Pinch-to-resize & virtualized-table fallthroughs for cross-browser console
preview: Deliver a resilient interaction pattern for small-screen and mixed-input devices across the Redwood Console: enable pinch-to-resize split panels and card containers, improve virtualized table stability, and add deterministic fallbacks for browsers/devices that trigger rendering jitter or memory pressure. The goal is...
text: Deliver a resilient interaction pattern for small-screen and mixed-input devices across the Redwood Console: enable pinch-to-resize split panels and card containers, improve virtualized table stability, and add deterministic fallbacks for browsers/devices that trigger rendering jitter or memory pressure. The goal is parity of interaction and visual stability across Chrome/Firefox/Safari (desktop + iOS) and Chromium-based mobile browsers while keeping keyboard and screen-reader accessibility intact. This ticket covers finalizing the prototype, capturing cross-browser test results, documenting engineering handoff, and gating acceptance on performance and accessibility metrics.

title: Mercator: cooperative preemption and KV backpressure shaping for latency-safe continuous batching
preview: Goal: implement a cooperative preemption policy (Mercator) that reduces hard interrupts while preserving tenant SLOs under heavy KV-residency and GPU memory pressure. Mercator focuses on graceful yielding, predictive KV backpressure shaping, and selective spill-to-CPU for long-lived KV working sets. This ticket cove...
text: Goal: implement a cooperative preemption policy (Mercator) that reduces hard interrupts while preserving tenant SLOs under heavy KV-residency and GPU memory pressure. Mercator focuses on graceful yielding, predictive KV backpressure shaping, and selective spill-to-CPU for long-lived KV working sets. This ticket covers design, prototyping in the runner, benchmark validation, and a staged rollout behind a runtime feature flag.

title: Construct latency-utility frontier for stochastic batching and eviction-aware cache tuning
preview: Objective: build an evaluation harness and dashboard to map the latency-utility (quality) frontier across stochastic batching policies and eviction-aware key-value (KV) cache TTL/eviction strategies. We want a compact signal that captures model-answer utility (win-rate and instruction-following) vs end-to-end latenc...
text: Objective: build an evaluation harness and dashboard to map the latency-utility (quality) frontier across stochastic batching policies and eviction-aware key-value (KV) cache TTL/eviction strategies. We want a compact signal that captures model-answer utility (win-rate and instruction-following) vs end-to-end latency for representative production workloads so we can automatically detect and rank acceptable config variants for rollouts and automated fallback rules.

title: SLO fanout tolerance & proactive load-shedding dashboards for multi-region inference
preview: Goal: Design and ship a set of instrumented SLIs and Grafana dashboards that enable proactive detection of multi-region capacity constraints and trigger gradient-based circuit breakers and targeted load-shedding before customer-visible SLO violations. Scope covers token-level latency SLIs, SLO fanout tolerance metri...
text:
Goal: Design and ship a set of instrumented SLIs and Grafana dashboards that enable proactive detection of multi-region capacity constraints and trigger gradient-based circuit breakers and targeted load-shedding before customer-visible SLO violations. Scope covers token-level latency SLIs, SLO fanout tolerance metrics per-customer shard, region capacity fingerprints, and a compact playbook for automated and manual mitigation.

Why this is needed: Current dashboards show high-level token p50/p95 but do not expose shard or fanout characteristics that lead to cascading failures during regional degradation. We need telemetry that correlates per-request fanout (replicas/streams), effective batching window, KV cache hit-rate, and tail token latencies so the control plane can make shedding decisions with low false positives.

Success criteria / acceptance criteria:
- Instrumentation: record ...

title: Cross-Backend Trace Delivery Coordinator with Partial-Ack and Replay Strategy
preview:
Goal: build a coordinator layer in the OTLP/ingest path that can deliver spans to multiple tracing backends (Tempo, Jaeger-compatible vendors) with graceful degradation and a partial-ack + replay strategy when downstreams fail.

Motivation: current single-sink writes lead to either blocking (latency spikes) or all-o...
text:
Goal: build a coordinator layer in the OTLP/ingest path that can deliver spans to multiple tracing backends (Tempo, Jaeger-compatible vendors) with graceful degradation and a partial-ack + replay strategy when downstreams fail.

Motivation: current single-sink writes lead to either blocking (latency spikes) or all-or-nothing failures and manual remediations. We need a mechanism that: prioritizes tenant lanes, records durable progress per-sink, acknowledges only what is durably stored, and enqueues failed envelopes for later replay to a cold DLQ (S3) while preserving ordering and idempotency guarantees required by our trace stitching and query paths.

Scope and non-goals: this ticket is focused on the coordinator and delivery semantics (ack model, replay) and light integration shims for Tempo/Jaeger endpoints. We are not reworking sampling policies, UI trace search, or long-term retent...

title: First Run: Guided Capacity Tour & Autoscale Pattern Illustrations
preview: Design a first-run guided capacity tour for Dedicated that combines an explainer sequence (microcopy + illustrations) with an interactive capacity slider, autoscaling pattern visualizations (steady, reactive, scheduled), and an SLA overview card. Target audience: platform engineers and product teams during initial D...
text: Design a first-run guided capacity tour for Dedicated that combines an explainer sequence (microcopy + illustrations) with an interactive capacity slider, autoscaling pattern visualizations (steady, reactive, scheduled), and an SLA overview card. Target audience: platform engineers and product teams during initial Dedicated setup. Goal is to reduce setup time and SLA confusion while increasing correct autoscale configuration.

title: Terraform PR gates + OPA/Conftest orchestration for Private VPC deployments
preview:
Summary:
We need a standardized, gateable CI workflow for all Terraform changes that affect Private (VPC) deployments. This ticket defines the PR-level plan/apply pipeline, the set of static checks (lint, tfsec/checkov), and the policy-as-code gating layer (OPA/Conftest). The goal is to ensure safe automated upgrade...
text:
Summary:
We need a standardized, gateable CI workflow for all Terraform changes that affect Private (VPC) deployments. This ticket defines the PR-level plan/apply pipeline, the set of static checks (lint, tfsec/checkov), and the policy-as-code gating layer (OPA/Conftest). The goal is to ensure safe automated upgrades of Terraform modules, catch drift/unsafe network changes early, and allow deterministic rollout for Dedicated/Private customers.

Goals:
- Gate Terraform PRs with deterministic plan output and policy evaluations before any human approval.
- Prevent accidental open ingress, IAM over-permissive changes, or KMS misconfiguration from reaching prod Private deployments.
- Provide an automated upgrade job for module version bumps with canary apply and health-check rollback.
- Integrate drift detection and weekly health checks that surface via Console dashboards.

Design / Pipeli...

title: Compact NVTX naming and stream-linked kernel metrics for low-cardinality runtime traces
preview: Introduce a low-cardinality NVTX and span naming convention and attach CUDA stream-linked trace IDs so we can attribute per-kernel latency/CPU/GPU metrics back to user requests with minimal cardinality and overhead. Current NVTX labels are too verbose and high-cardinality (full op names + param suffixes) which flood...
text: Introduce a low-cardinality NVTX and span naming convention and attach CUDA stream-linked trace IDs so we can attribute per-kernel latency/CPU/GPU metrics back to user requests with minimal cardinality and overhead. Current NVTX labels are too verbose and high-cardinality (full op names + param suffixes) which floods exporters and makes aggregation expensive. This ticket designs and implements: 1) a compact canonical naming scheme mapping kernels to op families, batching mode, and quantization/precision; 2) propagation of a lightweight trace token onto CUDA streams so exemplar & span linking is possible for sampled requests; 3) a sampling strategy and reservoir to bound NVTX overhead for large requests; 4) exporter-side labels and histogram buckets for per-kernel latency, with exemplar trace IDs for p98/p99 latencies.

title: Introduce token-minting delegation service and audit-friendly token surface
preview:
Problem: We currently have a mix of long-lived root keys and ad-hoc service tokens that are hard to attribute in audit logs, difficult to rotate without customer impact, and incompatible with our SSO session model. This increases blast radius and slows incident response.

Goal: Design and implement a Token Minting &...
text:
Problem: We currently have a mix of long-lived root keys and ad-hoc service tokens that are hard to attribute in audit logs, difficult to rotate without customer impact, and incompatible with our SSO session model. This increases blast radius and slows incident response.

Goal: Design and implement a Token Minting & Delegation Service (TMDS) that issues short-lived, scope-limited tokens with machine- and human-readable metadata for auditability. TMDS should integrate with SSO/SAML sessions, support programmatic delegation (service->service), expose an admin surface for emergency revocation, and emit structured audit events compatible with existing logging pipelines.

Success criteria: (1) All new programmatic tokens are minted via TMDS by default, (2) Audit records show token provenance (issuer, parent token, SSO session id, requested scopes), (3) We can rotate underlying signing keys...

title: Retire dual-write flag pipeline and canonicalize rollout timeline
preview: Background: Over the last 3 years we introduced a dual-write pipeline that writes feature flag and rollout timeline changes to both the legacy 'compat' store (compat-rollouts) and the newer canonical rollout_store. The compat store exists to support legacy admin endpoints and older SDKs. Keeping both writers has inc...
text:
Background: Over the last 3 years we introduced a dual-write pipeline that writes feature flag and rollout timeline changes to both the legacy 'compat' store (compat-rollouts) and the newer canonical rollout_store. The compat store exists to support legacy admin endpoints and older SDKs. Keeping both writers has increased operational complexity (double failure modes), caused divergent state during partial failures, and slowed migration of rollout history queries.

Goal: Stop dual-writing, migrate any missing historical timeline rows into rollout_store, remove the compat-adapter codepath, and decommission the compat-rollouts dataset. After completion, all reads should be served from rollout_store and the legacy endpoints should return 410 or proxy to new endpoints per the API migration plan.

Success criteria (high level):
- Backfill job migrated all historical timeline entries for 99....

title: Bucketed commit tier simulator with telemetry gating probe
preview: Run a simulation-backed probe of a bucketed commit tier model that (1) groups reserved credit commitments into discrete buckets, (2) credits short-term streaming bursts via an on-ramp grace mechanism, and (3) applies telemetry gating to reduce gaming/abuse. This ticket scopes the simulation, acceptance criteria, req...
text: Run a simulation-backed probe of a bucketed commit tier model that (1) groups reserved credit commitments into discrete buckets, (2) credits short-term streaming bursts via an on-ramp grace mechanism, and (3) applies telemetry gating to reduce gaming/abuse. This ticket scopes the simulation, acceptance criteria, required artifacts for sales/legal preflight, and pilot plan for 6 pilot customers.

title: Region-aware fallback graph and automated deprecation workflow for pinned model versions
preview:
Problem statement:
Customers can pin to a model version (e.g., gpt-ax.v1) for stability. Today, when a pinned model becomes unavailable in a region (out of capacity), is deprecated, or fails runtime checks, behavior is inconsistent: some requests error, some fall back to unrelated variants, and others silently switc...
text:
Problem statement:
Customers can pin to a model version (e.g., gpt-ax.v1) for stability. Today, when a pinned model becomes unavailable in a region (out of capacity), is deprecated, or fails runtime checks, behavior is inconsistent: some requests error, some fall back to unrelated variants, and others silently switch to smaller models causing quality regressions. This causes production incidents, confused customers, and support load.

Goal:
Design and implement a deterministic, configurable fallback graph and an automated deprecation workflow for pinned versions. The system should: 1) prefer region-local compatible variants first, 2) respect customer pinning semantics (allow explicit opt-in to automatic downgrade), 3) surface deprecation notices and allow staged auto-migrations, and 4) integrate with published performance profiles so fallback choices minimize unit-cost and quality reg...

title: Heuristic orchestrator for kernel routing using GPU architecture and sequence-length fingerprints
preview: Problem: The runtime currently picks a single preferred kernel per model variant that doesn't account for GPU architecture differences (SM count, tensor core availability), or runtime characteristics like sequence length distributions for prefill vs decode. This results in suboptimal decisions: high-seqlen prefill o...
text:
Problem: The runtime currently picks a single preferred kernel per model variant that doesn't account for GPU architecture differences (SM count, tensor core availability), or runtime characteristics like sequence length distributions for prefill vs decode. This results in suboptimal decisions: high-seqlen prefill on Ampere GPUs picks kernels tuned for short sequences, and decode paths with KV-cache memory patterns pay a latency penalty.

Objective: Implement a lightweight heuristic orchestrator that routes inference requests to the most appropriate kernel variant based on a compact GPU \"fingerprint\" and sequence-length bucket (prefill vs decode). The orchestrator should be fast (microsecond-scale decision), safe (fallback to conservative kernel on uncertainty), and observable (clear telemetry for kernel choices and outcome).

Key ideas and constraints:
- GPU fingerprinting: capture...

title: streamline-sdk-dependency-graph-and-remove-legacy-promise-shims
preview: Goal: Reduce maintenance cost and runtime risk by consolidating SDK dependency versions across Python, JS, and Go clients, removing legacy promise/callback shims in the JS client, and cleaning up deprecated API surface. This ticket coordinates library bumps, compatibility shims, migration docs, CI changes, and rollo...
text: Goal: Reduce maintenance cost and runtime risk by consolidating SDK dependency versions across Python, JS, and Go clients, removing legacy promise/callback shims in the JS client, and cleaning up deprecated API surface. This ticket coordinates library bumps, compatibility shims, migration docs, CI changes, and rollout strategy. Scope: (1) Bump supported transitive deps across SDKs (httpx, aiohttp, node-fetch -> undici, protobuf, gogo/protobuf replacements), (2) Remove legacy promise/callback wrappers in JS client and expose unified async/await + synchronous helper where possible, (3) Run full integration matrix against hosted API and dedicated runtime to detect regressions, (4) Publish migration guides and codemods, (5) Prepare canary and gradual rollout plan with feature flags, (6) Update packaging and release tooling (build matrices, pinned GH actions). Out of scope: Large API surfa...

title: Warm-Standby Certification & Region Handoff Orchestrator (hopscotch method)
preview: Goal: Implement an orchestrator and certification pipeline that exercises multi-region failover semantics and validates warm-standby readiness using a staggered 'hopscotch' priming pattern to avoid synchronized cache churn. This ticket covers: (1) a controller that coordinates region selection and tier routing decis...
text: Goal: Implement an orchestrator and certification pipeline that exercises multi-region failover semantics and validates warm-standby readiness using a staggered 'hopscotch' priming pattern to avoid synchronized cache churn. This ticket covers: (1) a controller that coordinates region selection and tier routing decisions during outage windows, (2) an automated readiness scoring service that runs periodic probes and computes a single readiness_score per region+tier+model_variant, (3) an acceptance/test harness to certify warm standby pools before they are accepted as active failover targets, and (4) telemetry and alerting to detect degraded priming or variant incompatibilities. Context: previous warm-start efforts experienced synchronous cache invalidations when all standby pools followed the same priming cadence. The hopscotch method staggers priming across regions and model variants s...

title: Length Huddle: token-aggregator for runtime pad-shedding and microbatch consolidation
preview: Implement a runtime-level 'Length Huddle' token-aggregator that groups concurrent requests into transient micro-huddles by sequence-length proximity and request phase (prefill vs decode), to reduce padding and improve kernel utilization without adding significant scheduling overhead. The feature targets short-to-mid...
text: Implement a runtime-level 'Length Huddle' token-aggregator that groups concurrent requests into transient micro-huddles by sequence-length proximity and request phase (prefill vs decode), to reduce padding and improve kernel utilization without adding significant scheduling overhead. The feature targets short-to-mid tail workloads where padding dominates token cost and kernel occupancy is suboptimal.

title: Synthetic calibration generator and runtime drift guard for FP8 mode selection (E4M3/E5M2)
preview: Enable safe, automated FP8 (E4M3 / E5M2) rollout on Hopper by combining a synthetic calibration data generator with a runtime drift-guard that prevents cross-batch activation scale regressions. This ticket builds the calibration dataset pipeline, implements sentinel telemetry for activation variance, and provides a ...
text: Enable safe, automated FP8 (E4M3 / E5M2) rollout on Hopper by combining a synthetic calibration data generator with a runtime drift-guard that prevents cross-batch activation scale regressions. This ticket builds the calibration dataset pipeline, implements sentinel telemetry for activation variance, and provides a canary + rollback plan for staged rollouts. Goal: unlock FP8 savings without regressions in key quality metrics.

title: Welcome Companion: adaptive setup sequencer and contextual playbooks
preview: Introduce a lightweight in-product ‘Welcome Companion’ that adaptively surfaces a short sequenced setup experience based on user signal (account age, first API calls, chosen use case). The companion combines three UI primitives: a small persistent coach panel, contextual tooltips anchored to relevant console surface...
text: Introduce a lightweight in-product ‘Welcome Companion’ that adaptively surfaces a short sequenced setup experience based on user signal (account age, first API calls, chosen use case). The companion combines three UI primitives: a small persistent coach panel, contextual tooltips anchored to relevant console surfaces (dashboards, rollouts, cost views), and a library of one-click seed templates. Primary goals: reduce time-to-first-success, teach cost controls, and prevent confusion in empty states without adding modal friction.

title: Tenant embedding shift detector for zero-day workload changes
preview: Build an online detection service that represents tenant-level usage/cost/latency time-series as compact embeddings and surfaces zero-day workload shifts (novel cluster formation or re-assignment) with probabilistic alert scoring and noise-aware suppression. Goal: catch new behavioral regimes (engineered model chang...
text: Build an online detection service that represents tenant-level usage/cost/latency time-series as compact embeddings and surfaces zero-day workload shifts (novel cluster formation or re-assignment) with probabilistic alert scoring and noise-aware suppression. Goal: catch new behavioral regimes (engineered model changes, synthetic traffic, customer load pattern shifts) faster than threshold-based rules while keeping false positive rate low for on-call and product owners.

title: Automatic SLI Derivation and Cost-Constrained Trace Capture for Analytics Ingest
preview: Problem: Our analytics ingest pipeline produces a wide variety of per-tenant and per-route signals (ingest latency, parse errors, queueing depth, drop rates). Instrument owners keep manually authoring SLIs and choosing sampling policies for traces when things go wrong. That process is slow, error-prone, and leads to...
text:
Problem: Our analytics ingest pipeline produces a wide variety of per-tenant and per-route signals (ingest latency, parse errors, queueing depth, drop rates). Instrument owners keep manually authoring SLIs and choosing sampling policies for traces when things go wrong. That process is slow, error-prone, and leads to poor coverage during short-lived hotspots.

This ticket implements two coordinated capabilities: (1) automatic SLI derivation (ASD) that proposes candidate SLIs from existing metrics and synthetic probes using heuristics and supervised rules, and (2) cost-constrained trace capture (CCTC) — an adaptive, budgeted trace sampling controller that reserves trace capture for signals that matter to derived SLIs and anomalies. The goal is faster SLI onboarding, better signal-to-noise for incidents, and bounded observability cost for analytics ingestion services.

title: Cross-model streaming and tool-call semantics validation for pinned model variants
preview: Objective: Validate that every newly onboarded model (and pinned model variants) preserves streaming semantics and deterministic tool/function-calling behavior when published with a performance profile. Scope: run a cross-model test matrix that exercises streaming partial outputs, mid-stream function/tool call frame...
text: Objective: Validate that every newly onboarded model (and pinned model variants) preserves streaming semantics and deterministic tool/function-calling behavior when published with a performance profile. Scope: run a cross-model test matrix that exercises streaming partial outputs, mid-stream function/tool call frames, cancellation/retry, and the interaction with quantized/optimized kernels implied by published perf profiles. Deliverables: 1) Test harness integrated into CI that can run per-model streaming + tool-call scenarios; 2) A compatibility matrix (Confluence) with pass/fail and mitigation notes; 3) A canary rollout plan and a regression alert tied to the existing eval suite; 4) PRs that gate model onboarding until passing results are present. Acceptance criteria: - For each model variant in the onboarding batch, streaming must preserve exact token ordering for prefix+stream seq...
